title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jules and Gwendraith plan to travel to Anstey Hall in Dys, Northumberland, to investigate clues regarding Captain Sidney Erskine. To assist their investigation, Jules has written to Dr. Kennedy to request Helen Kennedy’s letters and a handwriting sample. Gwendraith suggests they attempt to locate Lily Abbott, a perceptive former servant from St. Catherine's Manor who once worked in Torquay, believing she may possess useful information. Giles agrees to place an advertisement to find her.

In Dartmouth, Miss Marple visits Mrs. Fenn, an acquaintance of her mutual friend Eleanor Fern. During their conversation, Mrs. Fenn discusses her three sons: Gerald, who works in Singapore; Robert, who is in the army; and Walter, who works at Mrs. Fenn's firm. Mrs. Fenn reveals that Walter once traveled to India to become a tea planter because he was in love with Helen Kennedy, a woman Mrs. Fenn considered unsuitable and promiscuous. According to Mrs. Fenn, Helen wrote to Walter claiming she would marry him, but upon his arrival at the docks, she rejected him to pursue a married man instead. 

Mrs. Fenn notes that Walter maintains extreme self-control and buries his emotions deeply. Probing into Walter's temperament, Miss Marple prompts Mrs. Fenn to recount a childhood incident in which Walter, after his brother Robert broke a model airplane he had meticulously built, attacked Robert with a fire stick while screaming that he wanted to kill him.
